observations.text: Three architecture decisions provided instead of the minimum two, including the RefundService bounded-context separation with clear implementation notes
Acceptance criteria includes four well-structured Given/When/Then scenarios, preserving ORDER_STATE_INVALID verbatim
Low confidence areas honestly flag gaps (incomplete transition matrix, unspecified error response shape) rather than papering over them
Assumptions section adds value — whitelist vs blacklist model and in-process vs HTTP call are genuine implementation decisions Shaq needs to know about

terminology_alignment maps domain terms to technical terms generically but does not address the specific rubric concern: that Bird says 'SHIPPED'/'order status' while MJ says 'state' in 'PATCH /orders/{id}/state', and that these refer to the same concept. The alignment entries are useful but miss the Bird/MJ language reconciliation.

Second acceptance criterion uses generic 'error indicating refund is not eligible' rather than preserving a specific error code like REFUND_NOT_ELIGIBLE, though this may not have been in the source material
